tagline: Ink and heat in equal measure.
instructions: Pour the rum into a warmed mug.
Add the hot coffee and brown sugar.
Stir until the sugar dissolves.
Drink it black — no cream, no apologies.
omen: The warmth is not a kindness; it is a negotiation. You will taste the char before the sweet, and in the morning you will remember something you had been trying to forget. Let it surface.

tagline: The current beneath every quiet evening.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the whiskey.
Top with cola and stir once.
Squeeze the lime wedge over the top and drop it in.
omen: There is no complexity here, and that is the point. The cola sweetens what the whiskey darkens. By the second glass you will have stopped explaining yourself to no one.

tagline: Served at the hour the fireplace dies.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake briefly until cold.
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice.
Squeeze a lemon wedge over the top.
omen: Tannin and molasses, conspiring. The tea grounds the rum the way old habits ground a restless mind. You will sit longer than you planned, and the sitting will be the remedy.

instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the vodka.
Top with cranberry juice and stir gently.
Squeeze the lime wedge and drop it in.
omen: Small fortunes only. The tartness catches you mid-laugh and that is exactly when it should arrive. Whoever texted you an hour ago can wait until morning.

tagline: One too many, and not the last.
instructions: Pour the orange juice into a flute.
Top slowly with sparkling wine.
Do not stir — let the bubbles do the work.
omen: You are celebrating something that has not happened yet, and that is the bravest kind of toast. The bubbles are counting down, though to what, neither of you can say.

tagline: A bright noise from a dark corner.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Add whiskey, triple sec, and sour mix.
Top with lemon-lime soda.
Stir once and serve with a straw.
omen: Clarity disguised as carelessness. The soda lifts the whiskey into something almost innocent, and for one evening you will believe that you have gotten away with it. You have not, but the evening will be worth the cost.

tagline: Two sevens and the truth between them.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the whiskey.
Top with 7-Up and stir once.
Serve without ceremony.
omen: No one orders this drink to impress. The sweetness is honest about itself, which is more than most things manage. Sit with that for a while.

tagline: A measurement taken at the clearest hour.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice to the brim.
Pour in the gin.
Add tonic water slowly down the side of the glass.
Squeeze the lime wedge and drop it in.
omen: The quinine hums. Beneath the bitter there is a botanist's garden you will never visit, but you will know it by its outline. Order a second only if the first one answered your question.

tagline: Sweetness that knows exactly what it is doing.
instructions: Fill a rocks glass with ice.
Pour in the amaretto and sour mix.
Stir gently to combine.
Drop in the cherry.
omen: The almond will remind you of someone's kitchen, and the someone will not be who you expect. Sweetness as memory — dangerous, imprecise, and impossible to set down once lifted.

instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the tequila and orange juice; stir once.
Slowly pour the grenadine down the inside of the glass.
Let it settle — do not stir.
omen: Watch the red climb. Something you dismissed as sentimental will prove to be the most accurate thing said to you this week. The orange is a disguise; the grenadine is the confession.

tagline: Built for the hand, not the eye.
instructions: Fill a rocks glass with ice.
Pour in the whiskey.
Top with ginger ale.
Squeeze the lime over the top and drop it in.
omen: The ginger is a dare; the whiskey is the follow-through. You will say yes to something before this glass is empty, and you will mean it more than usual.

tagline: Cold metal, sharp bite, warm consequences.
instructions: Fill a copper mug or highball glass with ice.
Add the vodka and lime juice.
Top with ginger beer.
Stir gently and serve.
omen: The ginger arrives first, a bright kick that asks nothing of you but attention. By the third sip you will have made a plan. Whether you follow it is between you and the second round.

tagline: Five spirits walk into a glass. None leave.
instructions: Combine all spirits and sour mix in a shaker with ice.
Shake briefly and pour into a tall glass with fresh ice.
Top with cola — just enough for color.
Serve with a straw and a lack of regret.
omen: Five currents pulling in one direction. You will not taste the ruin until it has already seated itself comfortably. This is a drink for the bold and the recently decided. Do not order a second unless you have cleared your morning.

tagline: A roof for the traveller, a fire for the bone.
instructions: Warm a mug with hot water; discard the water.
Add whiskey, sugar, and lemon juice.
Top with fresh hot water and stir until dissolved.
Drink slowly while something heavy leaves your shoulders.
omen: The cold retreats in stages — throat, then chest, then the long quiet behind the eyes. Tomorrow will be kinder than today, if only by a margin too small to name.

tagline: One by one the streetlights come on.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the rum.
Top with ginger ale.
Squeeze the lime and drop it in.
omen: A small warmth in each sip, the kind that asks nothing of you. The ginger hums in the background like a streetlamp you pass every night but have never looked up at. Tonight you will look up.

tagline: What the Navy knew and the officers pretended not to.
instructions: Dissolve the sugar in the hot water.
Add the rum and lime juice.
Stir once and pour into a warmed mug.
Drink before it cools — grog does not improve with patience.
omen: Salt-wind, tar-rope, and the promise of port. This drink was built for the end of a watch, and your day was a watch whether you named it that or not. Rest now; the tide changes in the small hours.
